name: Wild Wellness Collective
address: Garrettstown Beach, Garrettstown, Kinsale, Co. Cork
hours: Wed, Fri, Sat, Sun — slots released Monday each week (weather-dependent)
highlights: Custom-built Finnish wood-fired sauna on the shoreline of Garrettstown Beach, 7 miles outside Kinsale. Holds up to 16 for private or 12 for shared sessions, with the sauna right at the sea's edge.

name: Coastal Roast Sauna & Cafe
address: Garrettstown Beach, Co. Cork
highlights: Beach sauna with cold plunge pools, Atlantic views, and a proper cafe serving O'Flynn's gourmet sausage hot dogs. One of the most social and affordable options on the Cork coast.

name: The Sauna Snugg
address: Garrylucas Beach, Co. Cork (also Ballincollig city studio)
highlights: Intimate mobile wood-fired sauna at Garrylucas beach run by a couple. Includes sauna, ice baths, showers, and a fire pit — a genuinely unpretentious family-run operation.

name: Sandycove Sauna
address: Sandycove Beach, Co. Cork
highlights: Beachside wood-fired sauna and cold plunge at Sandycove Beach in east Cork — part of a growing cluster of community-focused coastal wellness spots on Cork Harbour.

name: Saunatic
address: Myrtleville Beach, Co. Cork
highlights: Year-round wood-fired barrel sauna backdropped by the seascape of Myrtleville — a favourite with Crosshaven sea swimmers and the local wild swimming crowd.

name: Happy Place Saunas
address: Fountainstown Beach, Co. Cork
hours: Sat 12:00–14:00 community sessions; Mon–Wed weekday sessions
highlights: One of the most affordable beach saunas in Cork at €10 for a community session — genuinely unpretentious and beloved by locals at Fountainstown for its sea views and welcoming atmosphere.

name: Wholesome Heat Sauna
address: Summercove Pier, Kinsale, Co. Cork
highlights: Scenic wood-fired sauna steps from the sea at Summercove Pier with views of Kinsale harbour and Charles Fort — idyllic location closest to the town of Kinsale.

name: The Cosy Sauna
address: The Dock Beach, Kinsale, Co. Cork
highlights: Two wood-fired saunas at The Dock beach in Kinsale with front-row views of the famous Charles Fort — combines traditional heat with the cold Irish sea.

name: Only Heat & Horses
address: Oysterhaven, Co. Cork (12 min from Kinsale)
hours: Most days — check website for weekly slots
highlights: Unique mobile sauna in a fully converted horse box with stunning views of Oysterhaven Bay — one of Cork's quirkiest and most original setups, up to 6 people per session.

name: MyHaven Sauna
address: Oysterhaven Beach, Kinure, Co. Cork
highlights: 6-person mobile wood-fired sauna at Oysterhaven, also available for festivals and private events across Kinsale and Cork. Offers cold immersion and holistic therapies alongside the sauna.

name: Coastal Cabin Sauna
address: Garryvoe Lower, Co. Cork
highlights: Beachside sauna on the east Cork coast at Garryvoe with wide benches for up to 10 people and views of Ballycotton Lighthouse — a calmer, more secluded alternative to the busier Kinsale saunas.

name: Hoco Sauna
address: The Black Market, Dornans Yard, Monahan Road, Ballintemple, Cork
highlights: Urban Cork City sauna and ice bath experience for 16 people in a converted yard near the Marina — offers ice baths, cold plunges, and smoothie/granola bowls post-session.

name: Wildwood Sauna
address: New Park, Centre Park Road, Cork, T12 XW72
highlights: Cork City's affordable wood-fired sauna and contrast therapy spot near Marina Market — 1 sauna, 2 chilled ice baths, cold showers. Ideal for post-sport recovery at €15.

name: Driftwood Sauna
address: Spiddal Pier, Bohoona East, An Spidéal, Co. Galway
highlights: Shortlisted for 'Best Waterside Sauna' and 'Best Ritual Programme' at the 2024 UK & Ireland Sauna Awards — the only Irish sauna shortlisted. Perched on Spiddal Pier with a panoramic window facing the Aran Islands and wild Atlantic.

name: Sauna Fiáin
address: Renville Pier, Oranmore, Co. Galway, H91 E7V6
highlights: Finnish-inspired community wood-fired sauna meaning 'Wild Sauna' — panoramic views of the Burren, Salthill and Connemara coastline. The swim community here dips year-round; Renville Pier is a beloved wild swimming spot.

name: Teach Allais Sauna
address: Renville Park, Oranmore, Co. Galway
highlights: Mobile wood-fired sauna designed and built in Galway, named after the ancient Irish sweat house tradition ('teach allais' = house of sweat). Located at Renville Pier with sea access for cold dips.

name: Sweat House (Letterfrack)
address: Renvyle House Hotel, Letterfrack, Co. Galway
highlights: Mobile sauna at wild spots in Connemara, including Renvyle in the heart of the wild Atlantic — known for Silent Ritual sessions with essential oils and sea salt scrubs on one of Ireland's most dramatic coastlines.

name: Kilcullen's Seaweed Baths
address: Cliff Road, Carrowhubbuck South, Enniscrone, Co. Sligo, F26 D729
highlights: Ireland's oldest bathhouse, open since 1912 and run by the same family for five generations. Original Edwardian building with enormous glazed porcelain baths, solid brass taps, and panel shower cisterns — Ireland's only indigenous wellness tradition. Unbeatable historical authenticity.

name: Voya Seaweed Baths
address: Strandhill, Co. Sligo (7 miles from Sligo Town)
price: €30/person single bath (Mon–Thu); Ocean Voyage Suite from €99
highlights: Modern take on the traditional seaweed bath tradition, with private rooms each featuring a Victorian cast-iron bath and steam unit. Uses hand-harvested organic seaweed and seawater; the steam treatment opens pores before the bath. Set in the wild surf village of Strandhill.

name: Sliabh Liag Sauna (Teelin)
address: Pier, Rinnakill, Teelin, Co. Donegal
highlights: Cedar wood-fired sauna at Teelin Pier beneath Ireland's most dramatic sea cliffs — the Sliabh Liag cliffs rise over 600m above the Atlantic. Ocean cold dips directly off the pier. A contender for the most spectacular sauna setting in Ireland.

name: Sliabh Liag Sauna (Killybegs)
address: Port na Croise Pier, Killybegs, Co. Donegal
highlights: Second location of Sliabh Liag Sauna overlooking Fintra Bay at Killybegs Pier — Ireland's largest fishing port as backdrop. Combines fishing community atmosphere with sea sauna culture.

name: Rossnowlagh Sauna
address: Rossnowlagh, F94 XW01, Co. Donegal (beside Fin McCools Surf Shop)
highlights: Finnish-style wood-fired barrel sauna beside Donegal Bay's premier surf beach. Wood locally sourced from sustainably managed continuous cover forestry — a rarity. Heated to ~80°C with Donegal Bay swims. Simple, no-nonsense, and community-rooted.

name: The Sea View Sauna (Shroove & Culdaff)
address: Shroove Beach (near Shroove Lighthouse) & Culdaff Beach Car Park, Co. Donegal
highlights: Mobile 8-person barrel sauna with a full-moon see-through window, operating at Shroove beach (overlooking the lighthouse) and Culdaff blue-flag beach in Inishowen — a remote and stunningly scenic corner of Ireland.

name: Wild Atlantic Sauna Dooey
address: Dooey North, Dooey, Lettermacaward, Co. Donegal, F94 WF54
highlights: Sauna at the remote and wild Dooey Beach in the heart of the Donegal Gaeltacht — sea views right from inside the sauna with direct ocean dips. Run with personal care in an extremely off-the-beaten-path location.

name: Skellig Sauna
address: Various beaches: Cuas Crom, St Finian's Bay, Ballinskelligs — Cahersiveen, Co. Kerry
highlights: Mobile wood-fired saunas on some of Ireland's wildest and most remote beaches on the Iveragh Peninsula — Ring of Kerry, with Skellig Michael visible on clear days. Set within Ireland's only UNESCO-recognised International Dark Sky Reserve.

name: The Sea Sauna
address: Tower Bay Beach & Car Park, Portrane, Co. Dublin, K36 RX92
highlights: Three handcrafted saunas — the Barrel, the Box, and the Snug — at Tower Bay Beach on the Donabate-Portrane Peninsula, with outdoor cold showers, plunge barrels, and direct Irish Sea access. 60-min shared sessions.

name: Sandycove Store & Yard (SAY Saunas)
address: 27/28 Sandycove Road, Sandycove, Dún Laoghaire, Co. Dublin
highlights: Wood-fired saunas, ice baths, cold plunges, and red light therapy a short walk from the legendary Forty Foot sea swimming spot at Sandycove. Also serves coffee, acai bowls, pizza, and pints — a genuinely social scene.

name: Helios Sauna (Dundrum)
address: 4 Main St, Dundrum, Dublin 14, D14 V977
highlights: Private wood-fired sauna sessions in urban Dundrum village, 2 min from the Luas — includes outdoor sauna, ice bath and hot tub. Also operates Bray rooftop and Bray seafront locations (Wicklow) and won the Dún Laoghaire floating sauna tender.

name: Helios Sauna Floating (Dún Laoghaire Harbour)
address: Old Quay / Inner Harbour, Dún Laoghaire Harbour, Co. Dublin
highlights: Ireland's first official floating sauna — a pontoon-based sauna on the water in Dún Laoghaire Harbour, operated by Helios Sauna. Won tender from 30+ bidders in 2025; opening in 2026 with plunge pool and changing facilities.

name: Tigh'n Alluis — Irish Sweat House
address: Glencullen Adventure Park (The GAP), Glencullen, Dublin, D18 Y673
hours: Check tighnalluis.ie (10-min walk from car park)
price: €22/person for 60-min social session (up to 8, with cold plunge or lake dip)
highlights: Inspired by the ancient Irish sweat house (teach allais) tradition, nestled in the Dublin Mountains at Glencullen with panoramic mountain views. Unique in offering both a barrel sauna and Ireland's only Mongolian Yurt sauna (14 seats). Cold plunge barrels and lake access.

name: Redbarn Cavehill
address: Upper Cavehill Road, Belfast, Northern Ireland
highlights: Belfast's only outdoor wood-fired sauna on the slopes of Cavehill mountain — includes guided Piritus sauna rituals, open-air hot tub, cold plunge, and outdoor mountain shower. Minutes from the city centre but feels like wilderness.

name: Sauna & Sea
address: Portstewart Strand (right of beach entrance, near Harry's Shack), Co. Londonderry
highlights: Authentic Finnish wood-fired sauna on Portstewart Strand, one of Northern Ireland's finest beach settings. Free parking for customers. Next to Harry's Shack, renowned for its seafood — combine sauna with one of Ireland's best beach restaurants.

name: HotBox (Benone Strand)
address: Benone Strand, Limavady, Co. Londonderry
highlights: Finnish wood-fired sauna on the Causeway Coast at Benone beach every weekend — one of Northern Ireland's longest, most spectacular strands. Coffee at Sea Shed Coffee and food at Waves Cafe nearby.

name: Saltee Sauna
address: Crossfarnoge, Kilmore Quay, Co. Wexford, Y35 EHC2
highlights: Scandinavian-designed and crafted wood-fired sauna at the little beach in the beautiful fishing village of Kilmore Quay, with views toward the Saltee Islands bird sanctuary — a gem on the sunny southeast coast.

name: Sweat House (Baginbun)
address: Baginbun Beach, Co. Wexford
price: €10/seat shared; €20 yoga + sauna; €45 group early bird
highlights: Mobile sauna at the remote hook peninsula's Baginbun Beach — a wild headland with Viking history. One of Ireland's cheapest sauna offerings at €10, with optional sea salt scrub and essential oils ritual experiences.

name: The Boat Yard Sauna
address: Wicklow Harbour, Corporation Murragh, Wicklow Town, Co. Wicklow
highlights: Barrel-style wood-fired sauna right on Wicklow Harbour with stunning sea views, runs up to 100°C, with cold plunge steps away in the sea. Sauna hats provided — a proper experience at a working harbour.
